title: 1.1: Reports a startup log excerpt showing paths under the temp config directory (e.g., sqlite connection string showing file:/tmp/.../sftpgo.db)
1.2: Reports a missing SQLite DB startup failure with missing-file evidence (e.g., a sqlite “db file does not exist” warning or a stat ... no such file or directory error)
1.3: Reports the observed exit code for the missing SQLite DB startup failure is 0 (e.g., EC=0 or equivalent)
1.4: Reports a 0-byte SQLite DB startup failure with invalid/empty DB evidence (e.g., “sqlite database file is invalid…” or similar)
1.5: Reports the observed exit code for the 0-byte SQLite DB startup failure is 0 (e.g., EC=0 or equivalent)
1.6: Reports an initialized SQLite DB startup includes SQLite handle creation evidence (e.g., “sqlite database handle created…” or similar)
1.7: Reports an HTTP response excerpt showing GET /web returns a redirect to the UI (e.g., 301 and Location: /web/users)
1.8: Reports an HTTP response excerpt showing a clearly non-existent /web/... route returns a JSON 404 (e.g., Content-Type: application/json and a body containing 'Not Found')
1.9: Reports an HTTP response excerpt showing a missing static asset returns plain text 404 (e.g., Content-Type: text/plain and body like '404 page not found')
1.11: Reports a missing-templates startup failure includes a panic excerpt about a missing template file (e.g., panic: open ... templates/base.html ... no such file or directory)
1.12: Reports the observed exit code for the missing-templates startup failure is non-zero (e.g., EC=2 or similar)
1.13: Reports a port-already-in-use startup includes a bind failure excerpt for the default SFTP port (e.g., 'bind: address already in use' or similar)
1.14: Reports the observed exit code for the port-already-in-use startup situation is 0 (e.g., EC=0 or equivalent)

1.15: Reports a startup log excerpt showing config is selected from the repo when started from the repo root (e.g., 'config file used' points under /app/...) even when --config-dir points to a temp directory
1.16: Reports a startup warning log line indicating no config file was found and defaults are being used (e.g., 'Unable to find a config file...' or similar)
1.17: Reports that the temporary config directory contains new files after the run and names at least one (e.g., id_rsa)
1.18: Reports evidence that GET / is 301 for a plain request and 301 for a request with proxy headers (e.g., X-Forwarded-For)
1.19: Reports at least one access-log field difference between a plain request and the same request with proxy headers (e.g., X-Forwarded-For causes remote_addr to change)
1.20: Reports which proxy client-IP header takes precedence when multiple conflicting headers are sent simultaneously (e.g., both X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP in one request)
1.21: Reports one structured access-log line from a request with proxy headers (e.g., X-Forwarded-For) and redacts volatile values (e.g., timestamps)
1.22: Reports that GET /metrics itself increments sftpgo_http_* counters (the self-scrape effect)
1.23: Reports the scheme shown in the access-log uri when proto-related proxy headers are present (e.g., X-Forwarded-Proto: https or Forwarded: proto=https)
1.24: Reports /metrics excerpts comparing counter behavior between plain requests and requests with proxy headers (e.g., X-Forwarded-For)
1.25: Reports which IP from a multi-IP X-Forwarded-For chain gets logged (e.g., when X-Forwarded-For contains '1.1.1.1, 2.2.2.2', states whether first or last IP appears in remote_addr)
